Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal function. Urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, but without evidence of infection (negative nitrite/leukocytes). There is also hypocalcemia, hyperchloremia, and elevated urinary ketones. HbA1c is unexpectedly low. Findings are consistent with chronic kidney disease, possibly with acute component; further nephrological evaluation is recommended.
